text:
Awakening (, Kakusei) is the 219 th chapter of the Hunter × Hunter manga, written and illustrated by Yoshihiro Togashi.

Synopsis

Killua is leading the nearby Chimera Ant away from Palm and Gon on their date. Rammot then reveals himself, stating that he smelled a human and remembers Killua. He tells Killua that he will suffer the pains of hell and asks where Gon is, rightly deducing that since they are generally together, he must be nearby. He then says that he will just yell while swinging his severed head around in order to get Gon to come out. Killua, meanwhile, is glad he was able to lead Rammot away, but realizes that Rammot is right and that leading him away does nothing if he also can't defeat him.

Illumi's aura then numbs Killua's mind and tells him to run away. He tries to fight it, but the fear takes hold of him. This excites Rammot who tells Killua that he has to kill him now and that he will crush him into a bloody pulp. Killua tries to overcome his fear, but his brother's words ring through his head telling him to avoid battles he can't win. He screams trying to overcome it, but Rammot attacks him with a massive punch, sending him flying. Rammot becomes orgasmic at the realization that Killua is paralyzed by fear, telling himself that he wants to kill him but also savoring the feeling. He then punches and kicks Killua multiple times. Killua gets up thinking he must protect Gon since he is his best friend. With a surprised expression on his face, Rammot tells him not to look at him like that since it's making him want to kill Killua immediately.

Killua again feels Illumi's aura and is reminded to run away over and over again. Rammot runs over towards Killua to take off his head, which he wants to save while eating only the tongue. Killua still feels pressured to run away, but the thought of not wanting to lose to Rammot and his friendship with Gon slowly push this feeling away as he remembers all the great, wonderful and just normal times he had with Gon during their travels and adventures. Blood then squirts from Killua's head as he dodges Rammot's attack. Rammot is surprised by this, and Killua is seen bleeding from his forehead while he holds something in his closed fist. He opens his fist to reveal a tiny thin needle and tells Rammot that it has been inside of his brain all along, wondering when Illumi planted it there.

A confused Rammot asks him how he got away, but he remembers that he saw Killua stab his forehead with his finger before disappearing. Killua wipes his face on his sleeve and tells Rammot that he feels much better, now that's he's fully awake and free. Rammot then looks shocked and tries to calm himself by thinking that all Killua did was move out of the way, yet he now is the one that has become paralyzed by fear. As he looks at the murderous aura coming from Killua, he "sees" a giant snake (his hare part's natural predator) baring down on him. Killua says that Rammot should tell the other Ants that they can expect to end up dead if they ever come near them again. Rammot angrily shouts out against his threat, but Killua stops him midscream by instantly ripping off his head, which continues to have its mouth wide open. Telling Rammot to die and saying that he doesn't care, Killua then crushes his head into a bloody pulp with his hand before the body even gets the chance to fall to the ground.

Characters in Order of Appearance

Trivia

The chapter's title was reused for Chapter 382.
